event: Saint George Coming to the Rescue
symbols: Saint George on a white horse — the divine warrior fighting against evil, echoing Revelation's rider on a white horse
sword raised — the sword of the Spirit, the weapon of righteousness
armor — the armor of God described by Paul
white horse — purity and divine commissioning
description: A sepia-toned postcard illustration showing a mounted knight in shining armor (Saint George) on a white horse, charging forward with a drawn sword. The horse rears dramatically. Below, a larger portrait of a bearded military figure is superimposed. In the background, silhouettes of church spires and marching soldiers are faintly visible. Text along the side reads 'S. Georges a la Rescousse' (St. George Coming to the Rescue). The image combines hagiographic imagery with wartime propaganda.
theological_description: Saint George, the legendary dragon-slaying warrior saint, became a powerful symbol of Christian courage against evil. Though his story is not biblical, the image of the mounted warrior resonates deeply with Revelation's vision of Christ: 'I saw heaven opened, and behold, a white horse! The one sitting on it is called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he judges and makes war' (REV.19.11). The armor evokes Paul's exhortation to 'put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil' (EPH.6.11). George's rescue mission reflects the biblical pattern of God sending deliverers — from the judges of Israel to Christ himself, who came 'to seek and to save the lost' (LUK.19.10). This wartime adaptation transforms the spiritual warrior into a national champion, yet the underlying theology remains: the battle against evil requires divine empowerment and righteous courage.

scripture_refs.reason: The rider on the white horse, Faithful and True, whom Saint George prefigures
The armor of God, visualized in George's military equipment
Christ came to seek and save the lost, the rescue mission George embodies

_meta.title: St. George, coming to the rescue
